4 min read
2 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

What if you could send a request body without pretending your read operation is a POST?
For years, web developers have been forced into an awkward compromise.
A simple search endpoint starts as a clean GET request.
GET /api/items?category=laptopsEverything works perfectly.
Then product managers add more filters.

• Multiple categories

• Price ranges

• Nested sorting options

• Complex search conditions

• User preferences
Suddenly your URL looks like this:
GET /api/items?
category=laptops&
brand=apple&
price[min]=500&
price[max]=3000&
availability=in-stock&
sort=rating&
...Eventually, the URL becomes too large or simply too difficult to maintain.
Most teams switch to POST.
POST /api/items/searchwith
{
"category": "laptops",
"brands": ["Apple", "Dell"],
"price": {
"min": 500,
"max": 3000
}
}
• Problem solved?

• Not exactly.
